The smooth stones of the ravine — those are your portion.
Your lot.
To them you have poured your drink offerings;
to them you have brought your grain.
Should I relent because of these things?
57.7:
On a high and lofty mountain you made your bed —
you went up there to sacrifice.
57.8:
Behind the door and the doorpost you put up your memorials.
For you turned from me — you uncovered yourself.
You went up and spread your bed wide.
You struck your bargain with them.
You loved their bed;
you gazed on what was exposed.
57.9:
You went to the king with oil
and heaped up your perfumes.
You sent your envoys far off —
all the way down to Sheol.
57.10:
You wore yourself out with all your wandering,
but you never said, 'There is no hope.'
You found renewed strength —
so you kept going, not faint.
57.11:
Who was it you feared — who terrified you — that you lied and forgot me,
never turning a thought to me?
Was it because I kept silent so long
that you stopped fearing me?
57.12:
I will announce your righteousness — all your deeds.
They will not help you.
57.14:
A voice will say: Build up, build up — prepare the road!
Clear every stumbling block from the way for my people.
57.15:
For the high and lofty One says this —
the one who inhabits eternity,
whose name is Holy:
I live in the high and holy place —
and also with the one who is crushed and lowly in spirit,
to revive the spirit of the lowly,
to revive the heart of the contrite.
57.16:
I will not strive forever,
nor will I always be angry —
for the spirit would give out before me:
the very breath of life I created.
57.17:
I was angry because of his greedy iniquity.
I struck him.
I hid my face — I was angry.
And yet he kept going,
backsliding along the road of his own heart.
57.18:
I have seen his ways — and I will heal him.
I will lead him and restore comfort to him,
to him and to those who mourn with him —
57.19:
creating the fruit of the lips:
Peace — peace to the far and to the near —
Yahweh says it —
and I will heal them.
57.20:
But the wicked are like the sea in a storm —
it cannot be still.
Its waters churn up mire and mud.
57.21: There is no peace for the wicked — my God says it.

58.1: Cry out at full volume — do not hold back. Raise your voice like a trumpet. Tell my people what they have done wrong; tell the house of Jacob their sins.
58.2: And yet — every single day they seek me. They are eager to know my ways, as if they were a people who actually practiced righteousness, who never abandoned their God's commands. They ask me for just decrees. They claim to delight in coming near to God.
58.3: They ask: 'Why have we fasted if you don't see it? Why have we afflicted ourselves and you take no notice?' Here is why: on the very day of your fasting you are chasing your own pleasure — and driving your workers to the bone.
58.4: Look — you fast so you can get into arguments and throw punches. A fast like that will never send your prayers to heaven.
58.5: Is that the fast I have chosen — a day of self-deprivation? Bowing your head like a reed? Lying in sackcloth and ashes? Is that what you call a fast? Is that an acceptable day before Yahweh?
58.6: This is the fast I choose: loosen every chain of injustice. Untie the ropes of every yoke. Set the oppressed free. Break every yoke — every single one.
58.7: It is to break your bread with the hungry. To open your home to the homeless poor. To clothe the naked when you see one. Not to look the other way when it is your own family.
58.8: Then your light will burst out like the dawn. Your healing will spring up without delay. Your righteousness will march before you like a vanguard, and the glory of Yahweh will cover your back — as it did in the old Exodus.
58.9: Then you will call out and Yahweh will answer. You will cry and he will say: 'Here I am.' If you stop putting the yoke on people's necks, if you stop pointing the accusing finger, if you stop muttering malice —
58.10: if you pour yourself out for the hungry and meet the need of the suffering — then your light will rise in the darkness and your darkest night will blaze like midday.
58.11: Yahweh will be your constant guide. He will satisfy you in the scorched wilderness and give strength to your bones. You will be like a garden that is always well-watered, like a spring whose waters never fail.
58.12: Your people will rebuild what was ruined long ago. You will raise up foundations that go back generations. You will be called the Repairer of the Breach, the Restorer of Streets to Live In.
58.13: If you hold back from the Sabbath — stop doing what you please on my holy day — and call the Sabbath a delight, and the LORD's holy day something worth honoring; if you honor it by not going your own way, not chasing your own pleasure, not filling it with empty chatter —
58.14: then you will find your delight in Yahweh, and I will set you riding on the high places of the earth. I will feed you on the heritage of Jacob your father. The mouth of Yahweh has spoken it.

59.1: Look — Yahweh's arm is not too short to rescue. His ear has not gone deaf.
59.2: No — it is your own wrongs that have cut you off from your God. Your sins have made him hide his face from you, which is why he does not hear.
59.3: Your hands are stained with blood. Your fingers are soaked in guilt. Your lips have lied. Your tongue mutters its corruption.
59.4: No one is calling for justice. No one is arguing in good faith. They lean on worthless charges, they speak what is false. They are pregnant with mischief — and they will give birth to wickedness.
59.5: They are hatching snake eggs and spinning spider webs. Eat one of those eggs and you die. Crack one open and a viper crawls out.
59.6: You cannot wear a spider's web. You cannot cover yourself with something that falls apart. Everything they make is wickedness. Violence is what their hands are full of.
59.7: They sprint toward evil. They cannot wait to spill innocent blood. Wickedness is all they think about. Wherever they go, they leave devastation behind.
59.8: They do not know the way of peace. There is no justice in any of their tracks. They have twisted every road they travel — and anyone who takes those roads finds no peace.
59.9: That is why justice stays far from us. Righteousness cannot find its way to us. We wait for light — and what comes is darkness. We wait for brightness — and we walk in shadow.
59.10: We feel along the wall like the blind, groping in the dark as if we have no eyes at all. We trip in the noon sun as if it were midnight. Among the living, we are like the dead.
59.11: We growl like bears, the whole lot of us. We moan like doves. We long for justice — there is none. We look for deliverance — it is far away.
59.12: Our rebellions have piled up before you. Our sins stand against us as witnesses. We cannot deny our transgressions — we know our own wrongs:
59.13: We have rebelled against Yahweh. We have denied him. We have turned our backs on our God. We spoke oppression. We plotted revolt. We conceived lies in our hearts and gave them voice.
59.14: Justice has been turned on its head. Righteousness stands at a distance. Truth has collapsed in the public square. Honesty cannot find a way in.
59.15: Truth has gone missing. Anyone who refuses to go along with evil becomes a target. Yahweh saw it all — and he was appalled that there was no justice left.
59.16: He looked — there was no one. Not a single person willing to step in. He was astonished that no one was interceding. So his own arm brought the rescue needed, and his own righteousness held him up.
59.17: He armed himself with righteousness as a breastplate. He put on salvation as a helmet. He dressed in garments of vengeance. He wrapped himself in zeal like a war-cloak.
59.18: He will pay everyone according to what they have done: fury to his adversaries, judgment to his enemies — the distant coastlands included. No one is exempt.
59.19:
And so, from west to east — from the setting sun to the rising —
they will fear the name of Yahweh,
they will fear his glory.
For he will come like a flooding river,
driven forward by the Spirit of Yahweh.
59.20: A Redeemer will come to Zion — to those in Jacob who have turned back from their rebellion. Yahweh declares it.
59.21: And this is my covenant with them — Yahweh speaks it: My Spirit, which rests on you, and my words, which I have placed in your mouth — they will not leave your mouth, nor the mouth of your children, nor the mouth of your grandchildren, says Yahweh — from this moment and for all time to come.

60.1:
Rise up! Shine!
For your light has come,
and the glory of Yahweh
has risen upon you.
60.2:
See — darkness covers the earth,
and deep darkness blankets the nations;
but Yahweh rises over you,
and his glory appears on you.
60.3:
Nations will walk toward your light.
Kings will come to the brightness of your rising.
60.5:
When you see it, you will shine.
Your heart will pound with joy — it will overflow —
because the wealth of the seas is streaming toward you
and the riches of the nations are coming.
60.6:
A flood of camels will cover you —
the young camels of Midian and Ephah.
All of them coming from Sheba,
bringing gold and incense,
proclaiming the praises of Yahweh.
60.7:
Every flock of Kedar will be gathered to you.
The rams of Nebaioth will come as your servants —
going up to my altar as acceptable offerings.
I will make my glorious house more glorious still.
60.8:
Who are these — flying like a cloud,
like doves banking toward their home?
60.9:
The coastlands are waiting for me — Tarshish's ships are first in line —
to bring your children from far away,
their silver and gold with them,
for the name of Yahweh your God,
for the Holy One of Israel —
because he has made you beautiful.
60.10:
Foreigners will rebuild your walls.
Their kings will be your servants.
For in my anger I struck you —
but in my favor I have had compassion on you.
60.11:
Your gates will stand open without ceasing —
day and night they will never be shut —
so that the wealth of the nations may keep flowing in,
and their kings led in procession.
60.12:
The nation that refuses to serve you will perish.
The kingdom that will not submit — it will be utterly laid waste.
60.13:
Lebanon's glory will come to you —
cypress, pine, and box tree together —
to adorn the place of my sanctuary
and to make the place of my feet glorious.
60.14:
The children of those who once crushed you
will come and bow themselves before you.
All who despised you will fall at your feet
and call you the City of Yahweh —
the Zion of the Holy One of Israel.
60.15:
You were forsaken. You were despised.
No one walked through you.
But I will make you a glory that lasts forever,
a joy from one generation to the next.
60.16:
You will feed on the wealth of nations;
you will draw from the abundance of kings.
And you will know that I, Yahweh, am your Savior —
your Redeemer, the Mighty One of Jacob.
60.17:
Where there was bronze, I will bring gold.
Where there was iron, silver.
Where there was wood, bronze.
Where there were stones, iron.
I will appoint Peace as your governor,
and Righteousness as your ruling power.
60.18:
No more will violence be heard in your land —
no devastation, no ruin within your borders.
You will call your walls Salvation.
You will call your gates Praise.
60.19:
The sun will no longer be your daylight.
The moon will no longer be your brightness.
Yahweh himself will be your everlasting light,
and your God will be your glory.
60.20:
Your sun will never set again.
Your moon will not withdraw.
Yahweh is your everlasting light —
and your days of mourning will be finished.
60.21:
Your people — every one of them — will be righteous.
They will possess the land forever.
They are the shoot I planted,
the work of my own hands,
so that I might be glorified.
60.22:
The least among them will become a whole clan.
The smallest will become a mighty nation.
I, Yahweh, will bring it to pass —
and I will hasten it when the time comes.

61.1:
The Spirit of the Lord GOD is upon me —
because Yahweh has anointed me
to bring good news to the poor.
He has sent me to bind up the brokenhearted,
to proclaim liberty to the captives
and the opening of the prison to those who are bound,
61.3:
to bestow on those who mourn in Zion —
a garland instead of ashes,
the oil of joy instead of mourning,
the garment of praise instead of a spirit of despair.
They will be called oaks of righteousness —
the planting of Yahweh — for his glory.
61.4:
They will rebuild the ancient ruins.
They will raise up the former desolations.
They will restore the devastated cities — the ruins of generation after generation.
61.5: Strangers will stand and tend your flocks; foreigners will farm your fields and work your vineyards.
61.6: But you — you will be called the Priests of Yahweh; people will name you Ministers of our God. You will feast on the wealth of nations; in their honor you will take pride.
61.7:
Instead of shame — a double portion.
Instead of dishonor — joy in your inheritance.
You will possess double in your land.
Everlasting joy will be yours.
61.8: For I, Yahweh, love justice. I hate robbery and iniquity. I will faithfully give them their reward, and I will make an everlasting covenant with them.
61.9: Their descendants will be known among the nations, their offspring among the peoples. All who see them will acknowledge: these are the people Yahweh has blessed.
61.11: As the earth brings forth its growth, as a garden makes its seeds spring up — so the Lord GOD will cause righteousness and praise to spring up before all the nations.

62.1:
For Zion's sake I will not keep silent.
For Jerusalem's sake I will not be still —
not until her righteousness blazes out like the dawn,
not until her salvation burns like a torch.
62.2: The nations will see your righteousness; every king will see your glory. You will be called by a new name — a name Yahweh's own mouth will speak.
62.3: You will be a glorious crown in the hand of Yahweh — a royal diadem in the hand of your God.
62.4:
No more will they call you Forsaken.
No more will your land be called Desolate.
You will be called Hephzibah — 'My Delight Is in Her' —
and your land Beulah — 'Married' —
for Yahweh delights in you,
and your land will be wed.
62.5: As a young man takes a young woman as his own, so your sons will take you. As the bridegroom exults over his bride, so your God will exult over you.
62.6:
On your walls, Jerusalem, I have posted watchmen.
All day and all night, they must never fall silent.
You who call Yahweh to remembrance — take no rest,
62.7:
give him no rest until he establishes Jerusalem
and makes her the praise of all the earth.
62.8: Yahweh has sworn by his right hand — by his powerful arm: I will never again hand your grain to your enemies for food. Foreigners will not drink the new wine you toiled to produce.
62.9: Those who harvest it will eat it and praise Yahweh. Those who gather it in will drink it in the courts of my sanctuary.
62.10:
Go through, go through the gates!
Prepare the way for the people!
Build up the highway — clear it of stones!
Lift a banner over the nations!
62.11: Yahweh has proclaimed to the ends of the earth — say to daughter Zion: Look, your salvation is coming! His reward is with him; his recompense goes before him.
62.12: They will be called the Holy People — the Redeemed of Yahweh. And you will be called Sought Out: A City Not Forsaken.

63.1:
Who is this coming from Edom —
garments dyed deep red from Bozrah —
this one glorious in his attire,
striding forward in his great might?
'I am the one who speaks in righteousness —
mighty to save.'
63.2:
'Why are your garments red —
your clothes like someone who treads in the winepress?'
63.4:
The day of vengeance was in my heart —
and the year of my redeemed had come.
63.5:
I looked — but there was no one to help.
I was appalled that no one sustained me.
So my own arm brought me salvation;
my own fury upheld me.'
63.7:
I will recount the steadfast lovingkindnesses of Yahweh —
the praises of Yahweh —
for all that Yahweh has done for us:
the great goodness he showed to the house of Israel,
bestowed according to his compassion,
according to the abundance of his faithful love.
63.8: He said, 'Surely these are my people — children who will not prove false.' And so he became their Savior.
63.9:
In all their affliction, he too was afflicted.
The angel of his presence saved them.
In his love and compassion he redeemed them;
he lifted them up and carried them
through all the days of old.
63.10: But they rebelled and grieved his Holy Spirit — and he turned to be their enemy; he himself fought against them.
63.11: Then he remembered the ancient days — the time of Moses and his people. Where is the one who brought them up from the sea with the shepherd of his flock? Where is the one who placed his Holy Spirit in their midst —
63.12: who led Moses by his glorious right arm — who divided the waters before them to win for himself an everlasting name —
63.13: who led them through the deep as a horse walks through the desert, never stumbling —
63.14:
as cattle go down into the valley to rest.
The Spirit of Yahweh gave them rest.
So you led your people —
to make for yourself a glorious name.
63.15:
Look down from heaven and see —
from your holy and glorious dwelling.
Where is your zeal?
Where is your might?
The yearning of your compassion and mercy toward me — it is withheld.
63.16:
You are our Father —
even if Abraham does not know us,
even if Israel does not recognize us.
You, Yahweh, are our Father.
Our Redeemer from of old — that is your name.
63.17:
Yahweh — why do you let us wander from your ways?
Why do you harden our heart so we no longer fear you?
Turn back — for the sake of your servants,
the tribes of your inheritance.
63.18: Your holy people held your sanctuary for only a little while — now our adversaries have trampled it down.
63.19: We have become like those you never ruled — like those who were never called by your name.

64.1:
Oh, that you would tear the heavens open and come down —
that the mountains would shudder at your presence —
64.2:
as fire sets the dry wood ablaze,
as fire makes water boil —
make your name known to your enemies!
Let the nations tremble before you!
64.3: When you did those awesome things we never expected — you came down! The mountains shuddered at your presence.
64.4:
From of old no one has heard it —
no ear perceived it,
no eye has seen a God besides you
who acts for those who wait for him.
64.5:
You come to meet the one who rejoices in doing right,
who remembers you in your ways.
You were angry — and we sinned.
We have long continued in our sins.
Can we still be saved?
64.6:
We have all become like the unclean.
All our righteous deeds are like a filthy garment.
We fade like a leaf —
and our iniquities, like a wind, carry us away.
64.7:
No one calls on your name;
no one stirs himself to take hold of you.
For you have hidden your face from us
and given us over to the power of our iniquities.
64.8:
And yet — you are our Father, Yahweh.
We are the clay; you are our potter.
We are all the work of your hands.
64.9:
Do not be furious beyond measure, Yahweh.
Do not remember iniquity forever.
Look — we are all your people.
64.11:
Our holy and glorious house —
where our fathers praised you —
has been burned to the ground.
All our cherished places are laid waste.
64.12:
Will you hold yourself back in the face of all this, Yahweh?
Will you keep silent while you afflict us so grievously?

65.1:
I was there to be sought by those who never asked for me.
I was found by those who never looked.
To a nation that had not called on my name, I said:
Here I am. Here I am.
65.2: All day long I held out my hands to a people who would not come — walking a road that leads nowhere, following plans of their own making.
65.3: A people who provoke me — continually, right to my face — sacrificing in garden shrines, burning incense on unauthorized brick altars.
65.4: They haunt the tombs, spending nights in hidden places. They eat pork. Their bowls hold a stew of unclean things.
65.5:
Who say: Stay back — don't touch me, I am too holy for you.
These people are smoke in my nostrils —
a fire that burns without stopping.
65.6:
It is written before me. I will not be silent.
I will repay — I will pay it right into their laps.
65.7:
Your iniquities and your fathers' iniquities together — Yahweh declares —
because they burned incense on the mountains
and defied me on the hills,
I will measure out full payment for their former deeds —
right into their laps.
65.8: Yahweh says this: As new wine is found in a cluster — and someone says, don't destroy it, there is a blessing in it — so I will act for my servants' sake. I will not destroy them all.
65.9: I will raise up from Jacob a people, and from Judah an heir for my mountains. My chosen will possess it. My servants will live there.
65.10: Sharon will be a meadow for flocks. The Valley of Achor — once the valley of trouble — will be a resting place for cattle. All for my people who have sought me.
65.12: I will condemn you to the sword — you will all go down for slaughter — because when I called, you did not answer. When I spoke, you did not listen. You chose evil in my sight. You chose what gives me no delight.
65.13:
Therefore the Lord Yahweh says this:
My servants will eat — you will go hungry.
My servants will drink — you will be thirsty.
My servants will rejoice — you will be put to shame.
65.14:
My servants will sing out of hearts full to overflowing.
You will cry out in heartache.
You will wail in the breaking of your spirit.
65.15: Your name will become a curse-word left behind for my chosen — the Lord Yahweh will put you to death — but his servants he will call by a new name.
65.16:
Whoever blesses himself in the land will do so by the God of faithfulness.
Whoever swears an oath in the land will do so by the God of faithfulness.
For the former troubles have been forgotten —
hidden from my eyes.
65.17:
For look — I am creating new heavens and a new earth.
The former things will not be remembered.
They will not rise to mind.
65.18:
Be glad — rejoice forever — in what I am creating.
I am making Jerusalem pure joy,
her people the source of delight.
65.19:
I will take joy in Jerusalem and delight in my people.
The sound of weeping will no longer be heard there —
no voice of distress.
65.20:
No infant will die within days.
No old man will fail to complete his years.
In this new age, the one who dies at a hundred will be counted a youth —
the sinner cut off at a hundred will be accursed, dying far too soon.
65.21: They will build houses and live in them. They will plant vineyards and eat their fruit.
65.22:
They will not build for someone else to live in.
They will not plant for someone else to eat.
My people will have days like the days of a tree — long and full.
My chosen will enjoy the full fruit of their work.
65.23:
They will not labor for nothing.
They will not give birth only to see their children destroyed.
They are the offspring of Yahweh's blessed —
and their descendants with them.
65.24:
Before they call, I will answer.
While they are still speaking, I will hear.
65.25:
The wolf and the lamb will graze side by side.
The lion will eat straw like the ox.
Dust will be the serpent's food.
Nothing will hurt or destroy
on all my holy mountain —
Yahweh says it.

66.1: Yahweh says this: Heaven is my throne. Earth is my footstool. What house could you build for me? What place would serve as my rest?
66.2: My hand made all these things — all these things came to be — Yahweh declares. But this is the one I look to: the one who is poor in spirit, contrite, trembling at my word.
66.3:
To slaughter an ox — as if killing a man.
To sacrifice a lamb — as if breaking a dog's neck.
To bring a grain offering — as if offering pig's blood.
To burn frankincense — as if blessing an idol.
These people chose their own ways.
Their whole being delights in their abominations.
66.4: So I will choose what they fear most and bring it on them — because when I called, no one answered. When I spoke, no one listened. They chose evil in my sight. They chose what gives me no delight.
66.5:
You who tremble at his word — hear the word of Yahweh:
Your own brothers who hate you and drive you out because of my name
have said — Let Yahweh be glorified, so we can see your joy!
But it is they who will end up in shame.
66.6:
A roar from the city!
A voice from the temple!
The voice of Yahweh — repaying his enemies in full.
66.7: Before she was in labor, she gave birth. Before her pains came, she delivered a son.
66.8:
Who has ever heard of such a thing?
Who has seen anything like this?
Can a land be delivered in a single day?
Can a nation be born all at once?
And yet — the moment Zion went into labor, she gave birth to her children.
66.9: Would I bring a child to the brink of birth and then not deliver? Yahweh asks. Would I — who open the womb — then shut it? your God asks.
66.10:
Rejoice with Jerusalem!
Be glad for her — all who love her!
Rejoice in her joy —
all who mourn over her!
66.11:
Nurse and be satisfied at her comforting breast.
Drink deep — be filled with delight —
from her overflowing glory.
66.12:
For Yahweh says this:
I am extending peace to her like a river —
the glory of the nations like a flooding stream.
You will nurse;
you will be carried on the hip
and bounced on the knee.
66.13: As a mother comforts her child — so I will comfort you. You will be comforted in Jerusalem.
66.14:
You will see it — and your heart will overflow with joy.
Your bones will flourish like tender grass.
The hand of Yahweh will be known by his servants —
but his fury will fall on his enemies.
66.15:
Look — Yahweh is coming in fire.
His chariots like a whirlwind.
He comes to repay in burning fury —
his rebuke in flames.
66.16: By fire and by sword Yahweh will execute judgment upon all flesh — and those he slays will be many.
66.17: Those who consecrate and purify themselves to enter the gardens — following the one in their midst — eating pork and the detestable thing and mice: they will all be swept away together. Yahweh declares it.
66.18: I know their works. I know their thoughts. The time is coming to gather all nations, all tongues — and they will come and see my glory.
66.19: I will set a sign among them. From the survivors I will send messengers to the nations — to Tarshish, to Pul and Lud who draw the bow, to Tubal and Greece, to the distant coastlands — to all who have never heard of me, who have never seen my glory. They will declare my glory among the nations.
66.20: They will bring all your brothers from every nation as an offering to Yahweh — on horseback and by chariot and in litters and on mules and on swift camels — to my holy mountain Jerusalem. Yahweh says it. Just as Israel brings a grain offering in a clean vessel to Yahweh's house.
66.21: And from among them — from these nations — I will take some as priests and Levites. Yahweh says it. The gates of the priesthood are opening.
66.22: As the new heavens and the new earth I am making will endure before me — Yahweh declares — so your offspring and your name will endure.
66.23:
From new moon to new moon —
Sabbath to Sabbath —
all humanity will come to worship before me.
Yahweh declares it.
66.24:
They will go out and see the corpses of those who rebelled against me.
Their worm will not die.
Their fire will not be quenched.
They will be a horror to all who see.
